findings.check: ATLAS heartbeat parse (health, GI, anomalies, EVE tension)
findings.detail: Reviewed 20260325T090616Z.json: health=degraded, composite=0.701, anomalies=2, gi=0.83 mode=green, eve.global_tension=high, dominant_region=Global. Endpoints micro_agents/integrity/eve_news all true.

Live run (2026-03-25T10:01:15Z): composite 0.71 (aligned with heartbeat 0.701). anomalies[] contains only one entry: DAEDALUS-µ Self-ping HTTP 401, severity elevated. GAIA/USGS present as severity watch (max M5.2, 34 quakes), not listed in anomalies — resolved from the strict anomalies list vs heartbeat count of 2. THEMIS reports persistent upstream error: data.gov API fetch failed.

Self-ping remains elevated 401 — persistent, stable (not escalating). Seismic watch vs prior heartbeat anomaly framing suggests de-escalation relative to strict anomaly list, though seismic signal still active. No new anomaly types vs heartbeat-era pattern.

source=mock — GI score is not production-trustworthy per API contract. global_integrity=0.83 mode=green, terminal_status=nominal despite EVE global_tension=high (see cross-check). signals.freshness=0.6 and signals.information=0.6 indicate degraded freshness vs a fully fresh stack; aligns with partial upstream strain (THEMIS data.gov failure) but mock driver dominates interpretability.

findings.check: Vercel deployment health (READY, ERROR in 6h, ignoreCommand)
findings.detail: VERCEL_TOKEN was not available in this environment; Vercel REST/CLI deployment state, recent ERROR builds, and ignoreCommand behavior for bot commits could not be verified. Production HTTPS endpoints (signals, integrity, EVE) returned 200 at verification time.

global_tension=high with geopolitical-heavy feed, while integrity-status shows mode=green and terminal_status=nominal — potential disconnect between world narrative and reported system state. Feed items carry 2022 timestamps (stale curated/sample data), so tension label may not reflect current events; self-ping and data.gov issues are infrastructure, not explained by EVE headlines.

eve_correlation: High global_tension aligns thematically with geopolitical items but does not explain Self-ping 401 or THEMIS data.gov failures. Stale EVE article dates weaken real-world correlation; GI green/nominal under mock source alongside high tension is the main inconsistency to track.
recommendation: Treat GI as unverified until integrity-status source is live in production. Reconcile anomaly counting (watch vs anomalies[]) with ATLAS rules. Fix or document intentional Self-ping 401; restore THEMIS data.gov path. Re-run ZEUS with Vercel API credentials to validate READY state, ERROR deployments in the last 6 hours, and ignoreCommand on bot commits.
